title: protium subserratum
side_effects: Protium subserratum can have side effects if used improperly, such as severe allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems. Prolonged consumption of its extract can lead to liver damage, nausea, and vomiting. In some cases, improper use of the plant can also cause cardiovascular issues, including increased heart rate and blood pressure. If ingested in large quantities, it may also cause gastrointestinal problems, such as diarrhea and stomach cramps.
